name: Dictator's Diplomat
description: At the close of Y171, the Klingon Emperor dispatched the D7N Dictator to address the Romulan Imperial Senate — but the Federation command cruiser Lexington intercepted her in the Neutral Zone.
specialRules: MAP: Use a floating map, but keep track of the distance the original xx30 hex row is shifted as a result of the map floating. Any unit which disengages in an illegal direction is considered to have been destroyed.
TERRAIN: All territory below the xx30 hex row (in direction D) is Tholian territory. Any ship entering this area, even if pushed there by tractor, is considered destroyed immediately.

DISENGAGEMENT: Federation units can only disengage in direction A. The Klingon ship can only disengage in directions B, C, E, or F; however, it cannot disengage by any means in direction B or C unless a Romulan crew unit has transferred to the Klingon ship. The Romulan ship can only disengage in directions B or C.
ALLIANCE: The Klingon and Romulan ships are allied; they cannot damage one another in any way (unless the target has been captured by the Federation).
VAK KALEEN: Vak Kaleen initially occupies one of the bridge boxes on the Dictator. Special rules govern his movement, injury, and capture (SH80.46-SH80.467).

victoryConditions.notes: Use Modified Victory Conditions (S2.201). The Klingons receive a 100-point bonus if Vak Kaleen disengages on the Dictator or Centaurii in directions B or C. The Romulans receive a 100-point bonus if Vak Kaleen disengages on the Centaurii in directions B or C. The Federation receives a 200-point bonus if Vak Kaleen is captured, 100 points if he is killed. Klingon and Romulan point totals are compared independently to the Federation total.
